votes.reason: The Python code accurately translates all Coq functions, preserving the logic for converting to lowercase, finding distinct characters, and checking the count.
The Python code accurately translates the Coq definitions, maintaining the exact logic for string conversion, lowercase mapping, distinct character filtering, and the final specification check.
The Python code accurately translates the Coq definitions, including the custom lowercase conversion, list operations, and distinct character counting logic.

The Python code faithfully implements the Coq specification. to_lower converts uppercase ASCII (65-90) to lowercase by adding 32. string_to_list converts string to list of characters. list_to_lower applies to_lower to each character. mem_ascii checks membership. distinct removes duplicates by keeping first occurrence of each character (when scanning from left, keeping c if it doesn't appear in rest). count_distinct_characters_spec checks if count equals the length of distinct lowercase characters. All functions produce the same concrete results as their Coq counterparts.

The Python code faithfully implements the Coq specification. to_lower converts uppercase ASCII (65-90) to lowercase by adding 32. string_to_list converts string to list of characters. list_to_lower applies to_lower to each character. mem_ascii checks membership. distinct removes duplicates by keeping the first occurrence of each character (when scanning from left, it keeps a character only if it doesn't appear later in the list). count_distinct_characters_spec checks if count equals the length of distinct lowercase characters. All these match the Coq definitions and will produce the same concrete results.

The Python code faithfully implements the Coq specification. to_lower converts uppercase ASCII (65-90) to lowercase by adding 32. string_to_list converts string to list of characters. list_to_lower applies to_lower to each character. mem_ascii checks membership. distinct removes duplicates by keeping first occurrence of each character (when scanning from left, keeping elements not found in the rest). count_distinct_characters_spec checks if count equals the length of distinct lowercase characters. All functions produce the same concrete results as their Coq counterparts.

Python mirrors the Coq logic: only A–Z are lowercased, string is converted to a list, duplicates are removed keeping last occurrences using membership checks, and the count comparison matches the Coq spec for all ASCII strings.

Python mirrors Coq: ASCII-only lowercase for A..Z, list conversion, duplicate removal by skipping earlier occurrences (using membership in the rest), and compares count to length. Boolean vs Prop aside, results match on all concrete ASCII inputs.

Python mirrors Coq: it lowercases only ASCII A-Z, converts string to list, removes duplicates keeping last occurrences, and compares count to the length of that list. Membership and equality behave the same for ASCII, matching the spec.
